description: We carry out driving tests, approve people to be driving instructors and MOT testers, carry out tests to make sure lorries and buses are safe to drive, carry out roadside checks on drivers and vehicles, and monitor vehicle recalls. DVSA is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Transport .

details.body:
We carry out driving tests, approve people to be driving instructors and MOT testers, carry out tests to make sure lorries and buses are safe to drive, carry out roadside checks on drivers and vehicles, and monitor vehicle recalls.

DVSA is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Transport.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: From today (12 May 2026), only learner drivers can book and manage their own driving test - part of a crackdown on exploitation by third-party services.
ordered_featured_documents.title: End of the road for unofficial driving test booking services

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Get ready to pass your driving test, find out why people fail, practise ‘show me, tell me’ questions and learn how to deal with nerves.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Ready to Pass?

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The rules for booking and managing car driving tests will change from spring 2026. Find out what the changes are and how they affect you.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Changes to driving test booking rules in 2026

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Rules for lorry, bus and coach drivers on how many hours you can drive, exemptions from the rules, and when the rules can be relaxed temporarily.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Drivers' hours: rules and guidance

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Sign up to get free text or email reminders before your MOT is due, or view or cancel existing MOT reminders.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Get, view or stop MOT reminders

image.alt_text: Photo of mechanics inspecting a lorry

ordered_featured_documents.summary: How to keep heavy goods vehicles (HGVs) and public service vehicles (PSVs) safe to drive, including daily checks, inspections, maintenance and record keeping.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Keeping commercial vehicles safe to drive (roadworthy)

ordered_featured_links.title: HGV and bus drivers and Driver CPC

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read about the types of information we routinely publish in our Publication scheme. Find out about our commitment to publishing in Welsh. Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information. Read our policy on Social media use. Find out About our services.

details.body:
The Chief Operating Officer is responsible for:

  • leading and developing colleagues in the Office of the Traffic Commissioner
  • supporting the Senior Traffic Commissioner, Traffic Commissioners and their deputies
  • improving relationships with stakeholders, including the devolved administrations of Scotland and Wales
  • making sure the Traffic Commissioners’ statutory responsibilities are fully considered when policy is being developed by the Department for Transport
